talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center / Synthesize Bio
talk_metadata.talk_title: What Do We Do Now That We've Machine Learned Everything?
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "There are four grand opportunities in AI for cancer"
Speaker noted: "The regulation is very clear in some places and pretty unclear in others"
Speaker admitted: "We don't fully understand why some patients escaped"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Generative AI models for drug discovery
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of validated biological constraints in generative model outputs

key_data_gaps: No clinical trial data presented for generative AI outputs

crispro_opportunity.description: Partner with CrisPRO to add mechanistic validation layers to generative models to address blindspots.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that generative models lack clear regulatory pathways and validation.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide computational validation against known pathways and resistance mechanisms.

unresolved_questions: How will regulatory frameworks evolve to assess efficacy of generative AI-designed compounds?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI in Pharma R&D and Regulatory Considerations
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have the first Class 2 FDA clearance of an AI applied to pathology"
Speaker warned: "If we put bottlenecks, we're just going to slow innovation"
Speaker noted: "Explainability may not be required, but validation with fit‑for‑purpose cohorts is"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Model trained on limited histology data may not generalize to diverse patient populations
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker admitted limited adoption despite superior information content
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some pathologists responded positively while many did not adopt

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Class C2 AI biomarker validation trial
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: AI‑predicted therapeutic response from H&E images
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling mixed cancer subtypes without stratification for model training bias
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Prospective validation biomarker for AI prediction
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No plan for monitoring model performance drift over time
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Insufficient power to detect misclassification impact on outcomes

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed reliance on a specific deep‑learning architecture without broader IP protection
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing broad label without biomarker‑selected cohorts may dilute signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that AI can predict EGFR mutation from H&E (scientific observation) but concluded that explainability may not be required for clinical deployment (contradiction).

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective trial data for Class C2 biomarker

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to generate explainable surrogate biomarkers that satisfy FDA’s fit‑for‑purpose validation without full explainability.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker emphasized that explainability may not be required but fit‑for‑purpose validation is essential.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can produce transparent mechanistic maps linking image features to molecular alterations.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate CrisPRO‑validated biomarkers into trial enrollment criteria to avoid dilution of signal.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker warned that broad enrollment without biomarker selection dilutes signal.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify high‑confidence patient subgroups for targeted enrollment.

cited_competitors.name: MSK
cited_competitors.context: partnered on first Class 2 FDA clearance of AI pathology tool

cited_competitors.name: NCI
cited_competitors.context: commissioning a workshop on AI evaluation and benchmarks

unresolved_questions: What level of prospective evidence is required for Class C2 AI biomarkers?
How should data drift be monitored post‑deployment in oncology trials?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: FDA CDER / Oncology Center of Excellence
talk_metadata.talk_title: FDA Frameworks for AI in Oncology Drug Development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "AI is embedded in regulatory submissions, clinical trial designs, diagnostic platforms"
Speaker noted: "Our draft guidance does not cover drug discovery or purely operational uses"
Speaker warned: "Data drift is a particular concern in oncology because standards of care evolve rapidly"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: AI models used for patient risk stratification in trials
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Model may miss rare adverse event patterns due to limited training data diversity
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker highlighted high model influence and high decision consequence as risk factors

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Risk‑stratified monitoring trial for Drug A
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Predict low‑risk patients for outpatient monitoring after dosing
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Using AI model as sole determinant without secondary safety checks
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Independent clinical safety biomarker to corroborate AI prediction
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No plan for ongoing performance monitoring or model retraining
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering if misclassification rate is higher than anticipated

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Guidance is advisory; sponsors may develop divergent proprietary frameworks that bypass recommended safeguards
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Sponsors may rely on AI for high‑influence decisions without meeting recommended validation rigor

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented a detailed seven‑step risk‑based credibility framework (scientific observation) but also said the process is not a pass/fail gate, implying flexibility that could be interpreted as allowing lower evidentiary standards (contradiction).

key_data_gaps: No OS data presented for AI‑guided monitoring strategy
Limited real‑world examples of post‑market model drift management

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop public reference datasets for AI model external validation as urged by FDA.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker called for public reference data sets for benchmarking AI models in oncology.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can curate and host standardized oncology datasets for community benchmarking.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate a human‑in‑the‑loop safety check alongside AI risk stratification to reduce model influence.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed model influence and decision consequence as key risk factors.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide decision‑support dashboards that flag high‑risk predictions for reviewer oversight.

cited_competitors.name: EMA
cited_competitors.context: Collaborated on 10 guiding principles for AI use

cited_competitors.name: NCI
cited_competitors.context: Commissioning workshop on AI evaluation and benchmarks

unresolved_questions: How will FDA enforce lifecycle monitoring requirements for AI models in practice?
What specific metrics will define acceptable model drift in oncology settings?

assets_to_track: AI risk‑stratification models for adverse event monitoring
